title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool by Anthropic that runs on Amazon Bedrock, enabling developers to build features, fix bugs, and automate tasks while keeping data within AWS infrastructure for compliance and data residency requirements. The guide covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance.
writeup: This guide walks through running Anthropic's Claude Code, an agentic coding tool for building features, fixing bugs, and automating tasks, on Amazon Bedrock so data stays within AWS infrastructure for compliance and data residency needs. It covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance. The takeaway is a configuration path for teams that want Claude Code's capabilities while meeting AWS-based governance and content moderation requirements.
